home.title: Uniformed America
home.subtitle: Digital Encyclopedia of American Military Uniformology
home.articleText: The independence wars in Latin America were defined by decisive battles such as Ayacucho, Boyacá, Maipú and Chacabuco, where patriotic armies transformed the political geography of the continent.

granadero.title: Grenadier coat
granadero.description: An emblematic piece of the military elite that combined discipline, prestige, and visual impact on the battlefield.

campaign.title: Campaign uniform
campaign.description: Designed for mobility and life in the field, with functional fabrics and a more austere silhouette.

officer.title: Officer attire
officer.description: Command dress reflected hierarchy, authority, and the European influences of the patriotic corps.

uniform.title: Grenadier coat
uniform.descriptionText: A striking uniform that symbolized the discipline of patriotic troops and their ability to operate with precision in the early battles of independence.

about.title: About the project
about.description: América Uniformada project — a digital encyclopedia of military uniformology.

uniforms.title: Uniforms
uniforms.description: Explore the evolution of uniforms that shaped the identity of the armies that fought for independence.

people.title: Characters
people.description: Meet the leaders, officers, and key figures that shaped the emancipating campaigns.

battles.title: Battles of the American independence
battles.description: These campaigns marked the overthrow of Spanish colonial rule in Latin America and transformed the continent's political map.

countries.title: Countries
countries.description: A historical look at the nations that emerged from the independence wars.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.description: Trace the key events that shaped the emancipation process across the continent.

library.title: Library
library.description: A collection of visual and textual references to deepen your understanding of military history.

search.title: Search
search.description: Find uniforms, figures, and campaigns through a visual and thematic exploration.
